chunks.page_summary: This document provides guidelines for retrofitting an Emerson 7829 viscometer with Rheonics SRV inline viscometers or SRD density meters, highlighting the benefits and installation versatility of Rheonics sensors.
product_names: SRD - Inline Density and Viscosity meter
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Rheonics SRV - Emerson 7829 Retrofit
Modified on: Fri, 1 Sep, 2023 at 11:22 PM

What products are involved?
(https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) SRV - Inline Viscosity Meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/), and SRD - Inline Density and Viscosity meter (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).

What is the purpose of this article?
This article provides guidelines on how the Rheonics SRV inline viscometer or SRD density meter can retrofit the Emerson 7829 viscometer in its different installation variants.

1. Introduction
Rheonics SR- sensors measure viscosity, density and temperature inline for process control and monitoring. The SRV measures viscosity while SRD measures density and viscosity, both measure temperature and allow repeatable and reproducible readings with a high accuracy.
The sensor probe is compact, light, hermetically sealed, and factory calibrated. It does not need re-calibration over its operational lifetime, however verification capability is provided in software that comes with the sensor to verify calibration periodically and update if needed in field.
Rheonics sensors are based on a balanced torsional resonator (BTR), this means that the sensing element vibrates torsionally and is connected to the outside world at a node. The patented BTR technology underlying Rheonics sensors have significant advantage over competitors in the market since a balanced torsional resonator allows the SR- sensors to be compact, light-weight of reduced dimensions, mass and unaffected by external vibrations.

2. Retrofit
At times users want to replace an existing viscometer or density meter from their pipelines due to malfunction, lack of spare parts or to try out Rheonics sensors and experience balanced torsional resonator technology.
In these cases, the user has an existing installation location for the former sensor and wants to install the Rheonics -SR probes in the same location/port. Rheonics -SR sensors favor retrofit installations due to the modular design of the probe. Rheonics offers flush, short and long insertion sensors with all types of process connections while ensuring consistency of performance across all these variants.
Varying the insertion depth and process connection, Rheonics -SR sensors can be ordered to retrofit almost all existing installations. Figure 1 shows the SRV-X4 (Flush Tri-Clamp ), -X2 (Short flanged) and -X5 (Long insertion flanged), all with customizable process connection.
Rheonics favors open standards and these also provide a way for customer to order an alternative sensor when they plan to replace Rheonics ones.

Figure 1: Rheonics SRV sensor - versatility in installation options.
key_facts: Rheonics SRV inline viscometers and SRD density meters can retrofit the Emerson 7829 viscometer.
Rheonics SR- sensors measure viscosity, density, and temperature inline for process control and monitoring.
The SRV measures viscosity, while the SRD measures both density and viscosity.
Both SRV and SRD sensors measure temperature and provide repeatable, reproducible readings with high accuracy.
Rheonics sensor probes are compact, light, hermetically sealed, and factory calibrated.
Rheonics sensors do not require re-calibration over their operational lifetime, but software provides verification capability.
Rheonics sensors utilize patented Balanced Torsional Resonator (BTR) technology, which makes them compact, lightweight, reduces dimensions and mass, and are unaffected by external vibrations.
Rheonics -SR sensors are suitable for retrofit installations due to their modular design and offer flush, short, and long insertion variants with various process connections.
Rheonics SR- sensors can be ordered as SRV-X4 (Flush Tri-Clamp), SRV-X2 (Short flanged), and SRV-X5 (Long insertion flanged) with customizable process connections.
Rheonics promotes open standards, allowing customers to easily order alternative sensors when replacing existing Rheonics sensors.
diagram_labels: Figure 1: Rheonics SRV sensor - versatility in installation options.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides technical details and retrofit instructions for integrating Rheonics SR-sensors into Emerson 7829 Viscometers for inline viscosity and density measurements.
chunks.extracted_text:
Note: Rheonics sensors are suitable for retrofits but care should be taken to comply with minimum installation requirements for the sensing element to ensure reproducible and repeatable readings. These can be reviewed in installation guideline articles for SRV (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-viscometer-srv/) and SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/).

3. Emerson 7829 Viscometer
This instrument, also known as Viscomaster, is used to measure viscosity, density and temperature inline. Some common installation cases are shown next, highlighting the simplicity to retrofit these units with a Rheonics sensor.

3.1 Flow through chamber for in-line or bypass lines:
Emerson 7829 viscometer is designed with a 1.5" cone seat connection for mounting in a standard chamber as in the next figure.
Notice how Rheonics SR-Sensor doesn't need an external temperature sensor since it has an in-built PT1000.

Figure 2: Emerson 7829 viscometer installed in a Emerson provided flow through chamber [1].

For this retrofit, if we need to measure viscosity, the order code for Rheonics sensor would be: SRV-X5-A124-B15C (15C is 1.5” Compression fitting e.g. Swagelok). Code for SRD if density readings are needed is: SRD-X5-A124-B15C.

X5 Retrofit Installation
A Insertion Depth: 160 mm
Connection: Thread
B Type: NPT 1.25"-11.5

Units: mm [inch]

Figure 3: Rheonics SRV retrofit in chamber.

3.2 Flange installation:
A well known design of Emerson 7829 Viscometer is with flanges. Rheonics SR- sensors can be ordered with most process connection as it is in the next example for a 2” ANSI flange. User should determine whether the same insertion length (A length in drawing) is needed.
Rheonics offers a more compact and light-weight sensor with repeatable and reproducible readings. Order code in this example would be: SRV-X5-A124-BA20B (SRV-X5-A124-2” ANSI 300lb RF).
key_facts: Rheonics sensors are suitable for retrofitting existing systems, specifically Emerson 7829 Viscometers.
The Emerson 7829 Viscometer, also known as Viscomaster, is designed to measure viscosity, density, and temperature inline.
The Emerson 7829 viscometer features a 1.5" cone seat connection for mounting in a standard chamber.
Rheonics SR-Sensors include an in-built PT1000, eliminating the need for an external temperature sensor.
Rheonics SR-sensors can be ordered with various process connections, including 2" ANSI flanges.
Rheonics offers more compact and lightweight sensors that provide repeatable and reproducible readings.
warnings_notes: Care should be taken to comply with minimum installation requirements for the sensing element to ensure reproducible and repeatable readings when using Rheonics sensors for retrofits.
Installation guideline articles for SRV and SRD should be reviewed for specific requirements.
For flange installations, the user should determine whether the same insertion length (referred to as 'A length' in drawings) is needed.

Figure 4: Side-by-side: Emerson 7829 and Rheonics SRV flange retrofit.

4. New installations
The Rheonics SR sensors have significant advantages in installation due to its modular design and low or zero wall effects. They ensure repeatable and reproducible readings if two basic installation criteria are met:
*   sensing element is fully immersed in fluid, and
*   avoid stagnation or dead zones around the probe sensing element.

Often this means that you can easily remove flow adapters from old sensors and install the SR sensors directly in the line or tank. It also gives you flexibility to install the sensor in tight spots where space is limited.
Visit installation guideline articles for SRV (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297659) and SRD (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000297660) for more information.

Pipe tany configuration possible
flanged
tri-clamp
long insertion probe
NPT thread
Figure 5: Rheonics SRV flexible installation.

5. How to order a Rheonics SR- sensor for retrofit?
a. Client should find the connection type needed, specify norm/standard and size in order page. A reference drawing if available should be included.
b. Use online RFQ pages for SRV inline viscometer (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srv/) and SRD viscosity and density meter. (https://rheonics.com/request-for-quotation/srd/)
c. Select connection code. Rheonics offers standard connections as in next table, X2 and X5 codes are good for retrofits since these enables users to select the connection needed.
key_facts: Rheonics SR sensors have significant advantages in installation due to their modular design and low or zero wall effects.
Repeatable and reproducible readings from Rheonics SR sensors are ensured if the sensing element is fully immersed in fluid and stagnation or dead zones around the probe sensing element are avoided.
Rheonics SR sensors offer flexibility for installation in tight spots and can be installed directly in the line or tank by removing flow adapters from old sensors.
Clients should identify the required connection type, specify the norm/standard and size, and include a reference drawing if available when ordering a Rheonics SR- sensor for retrofit.
Rheonics provides online RFQ pages for SRV inline viscometers and SRD viscosity and density meters.
Rheonics offers standard connection codes like X2 and X5 that are suitable for retrofits, enabling users to select the necessary connection.

chunks.page_summary: This page outlines Rheonics sensor order types and connection specifications, provides contact information for support, includes a disclaimer regarding the use of illustrations, and lists downloadable resources and references for Rheonics viscometers and density sensors, including retrofit scenarios.

specifications.sensor_order_X2_connection_type: Standard short probe with custom connection
specifications.sensor_order_X4_connection_type: Flush design probe with custom connection
specifications.sensor_order_X5_connection_type: Long insertion probe with custom connection. Insertion depth (A length) and connection type (B norm, size) also required.

key_facts: Rheonics offers various sensor connection types identified by order codes X1 through X5.
Connection types include Threaded (NPT or G1/2"), Standard short probe with custom connection, Tri-Clamp, Flush design probe with custom connection, and Long insertion probe with custom connection.
For the X5 long insertion probe, both insertion depth (A length) and connection type (B norm, size) must be specified.
Technical sales (info@rheonics.com) and technical support (support@rheonics.com) teams are available to assist with correct retrofits.
Downloadable resources include a comparison/retrofit guide for Emerson 7829 and Rheonics SRV flange, and a guide for Rheonics SRV retrofit in a chamber.
The page references external documents concerning Emerson's Micro Motion 7829 viscometer, Rheonics' SRV viscometer, and Rheonics' SRD density and viscosity meter.
